mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals impending economic contraction, triggering downward revisions to corporate earnings forecasts and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 45% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent weakness in housing construction and retail sales indicates a broad-based demand shock that reduces corporate revenue growth, depressing equity valuations over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1150 and RSAFS declines > 3% month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a supply-chain recovery phase expands capacity utilization and improves profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive mean difference when the trigger activates compared to non-trigger periods (p > 0.05), or the signal's hit rate falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained expansion in real gross domestic product directly correlates with rising corporate revenues and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month Spearman correlation between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.20, or forward returns are negative in more than 55% of quarters with positive GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index crossing the 250.0 threshold confirms entrenched inflation, forcing prolonged monetary tightening that compresses equity risk premiums and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity forward returns remain positive or neutral in more than 60% of instances following the CPI threshold breach, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with a stable unemployment rate confirms a soft-landing macroeconomic environment, supporting elevated equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 150k and UNRATE remains below 4.5%
failure_condition: The joint signal predicts positive returns in fewer than 45% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS in a multivariate forward return model.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating real GDP growth below 1.5% annualized during a late-cycle expansion triggers earnings recession fears, depressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate falls below 1.5% annualized
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal's directional accuracy fails to exceed 50% over a 5-year out-of-sample walk-forward test.
